groups.theme: Cost-sensitive users blocked by paywalls and unclear premium value
example_reviews: Everything but the introduction is paywalled. I do not need an app to notice what affects mood. Downloaded for the mental health resources, but will use something that offers a chance to text the benefits before charging.
Puts results behind a paywall after using for 14 days, thus making it useless.

groups.theme: Routine-based users who depend on reminders and flexible check-in timing
example_reviews: not getting notifications to answer the questions
Needs more flexibility in timing. User should decide morning, afternoon, evening hours. Entries outside the prescribed times may be entirely lost, as in not recorded.

groups.theme: Therapy-oriented journalers who need useful reports and shareable progress summaries
example_reviews: I've been using this app since 2018. You answer a few questions 3 times per day for 2 weeks, and at the end it gives you a printable report. It's been very helpful to track my emotional state and report to my mental health team.
The ability to visually track my symptoms has helped give me more objective insight into my depression and facilitated work during CBT sessions.

groups.theme: Privacy- and continuity-focused users who need secure access to historical data
example_reviews: These days the app often logs me out and disables the app lock. I would like to keep my data safe and I'm beginning to lose trust in this app.
Used to be good. Now you cant access your own past data. Great. 3 years down the drain.

groups.theme: Users who want more expressive mood logging and less repetitive questions
example_reviews: The question: Do you feel sad or down? Needs a third answer, like: I don't know how I feel.
The emotions that are available are rubbish and you cant add your own. Asks repetitive questions and no room for gray area.
